board.title: Improvement Board
board.empty: No improvement items yet. Add one to get started.

dialogue.title: Improvement Dialogue
dialogue.select_item: Select an improvement item to run a dialogue:
dialogue.none_available: No in-progress items. Move an item to In Progress first.
dialogue.step2: What is the impact on the team or product?
dialogue.step5: What's the first small step you can take?
dialogue.notes_placeholder: Capture key insights from the dialogue...
dialogue.guide_body: An Improvement Dialogue is a structured 1:1 or small-group conversation. The goal is not to solve the problem for the person, but to help them think through it using guiding questions. The copilot asks, listens, and reflects — not advises.

timer.title: Problem Time
timer.description: Problem Time is a timeboxed team practice from Management 3.0. The team allocates a fixed slot (e.g. 10–15 min) during a meeting to discuss one problem using a structured format.
timer.step1: Select one problem from the board.
timer.step5: Last 3 min: agree on one next action.
timer.step6: Update the board with the outcome.

learn.title: About Improvement Practices
learn.dialogue_body: An Improvement Dialogue is a structured conversation between a team member and their copilot (peer coach). It uses guiding questions to help the person think through a problem themselves, rather than being told what to do. Typically 30–45 minutes.
learn.copilot_body: A Copilot is a peer coach — someone who helps you work through problems and improve. Unlike a manager, a copilot doesn't evaluate you. The relationship is mutual and voluntary. Teams can pair copilots for the whole team or for specific improvement items.
learn.problem_time_body: Problem Time is a regular team ritual (often at the start of a retro or standup) where one person brings a problem and the team helps explore it using structured questions. The key rule: no solutions during the problem presentation phase.
learn.p1: Problems are team assets — surfacing them is healthy.
learn.p2: Copilots ask questions, they don't give advice.
learn.p3: Progress is more important than perfection.
learn.p4: Closed items should be celebrated and learned from.
